creator: fuz@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to Daniel Engberg from comment #5)

I don't think you understand how encfs works.

Sure you can put the VeraCrypt container into a file, but then it's still a *container* that behaves like a partition.  Encfs encrypts each file into one encrypted file with file names scrambled.  This preserves the directory structure and allows file-based backup and synchronisation utilities to work just fine.  Veracrypt and all the other container-based encryption tools cannot do this.


None of this changes that without the port, people who have encfs encrypted file trees cannot access them.  A replacement will not help them, unless it supports the exact same encryption format.
